front: Work: set in an unnamed African country ruled by the 'Big Man'; narrated by the Indian businessman Salim
back: A Bend in the River (V.S. Naipaul)

front: Work: the Big Man publishes a book of maxims that all citizens are forced to purchase; sends the Youth Guard to forced labor
back: A Bend in the River (V.S. Naipaul)

front: Work: Ferdinand (son of the magician Zabeth) attends school; Salim runs a shop with assistant Metty
back: A Bend in the River (V.S. Naipaul)

front: Work: a ruined monument inverts a quote from Virgil's Aeneid near the town's dock
back: A Bend in the River (V.S. Naipaul)

front: Work: a sign painter tries to escape the Tulsi family by getting his own home; meets wife Shama while painting signs
back: A House for Mr. Biswas (V.S. Naipaul)

front: Work: a pundit predicted the title character was born the wrong way and would 'eat up his mother and father'
back: A House for Mr. Biswas (V.S. Naipaul)

front: Work: the title character's father drowns after his son hides following a cow's death
back: A House for Mr. Biswas (V.S. Naipaul)

front: Work: the title character writes about 'Deserving Destitutes' for The Sentinel; gets a car as a Community Welfare officer
back: A House for Mr. Biswas (V.S. Naipaul)

front: Work: residents include the tailor Bogart, carpenter Popo (working on a 'thing without a name'), prophet Man-Man, poet B. Wordsworth, and gambler Hat
Work: in 'One Out of Many,' Santosh marries a Black maid to gain citizenship after immigrating to Washington, D.C.
Author: claimed no female writer was his equal; couldn't share Jane Austen's 'sentimental ambitions'
Author: this author's clashes with C.L.R. James are described in a biography by Farrukh Dhondy
